message:
Defer Portal mount via requestAnimationFrame in 3 components

Updates the Portal helper in screenshot-gallery, bottom-sheet,
and war-stories-map to use requestAnimationFrame before setting
mounted=true, instead of a bare useEffect(() => setMounted(true)).

The immediate setMounted(true) in a useEffect could trigger a
synchronous state update during the commit phase on some React
versions, leading to a brief flash or hydration warning. Wrapping
in rAF defers the portal attachment to the next animation frame,
after the browser has painted the initial render.

Each Portal now also properly cleans up by canceling the rAF if
the component unmounts before the frame fires, and uses an early
return (if (!mounted) return null) instead of inline ternary for
readability.

Additional change in war-stories-map.tsx:
- Removed unused `type { WarStory }` import from lib/content
- Map container: changed from fixed aspect-ratio (aspect-[16/10])
  to min-h-[750px] on mobile with md:aspect-[21/9] on desktop,
  preventing node overlap on narrow viewports
- Node layout: switched from flex-wrap to CSS grid (grid-cols-2
  sm:grid-cols-3 md:grid-cols-5 lg:grid-cols-6) for consistent
  node spacing across breakpoints, with overflow-y-auto on mobile
- Added animated dashed border ring on selected nodes (rotating
  360° over 4s) for clearer selection feedback
- Node labels: slightly smaller (9px vs 10px), added group-hover
  color transition for interactivity hint

Additional change in bottom-sheet.tsx:
- Removed unused `cn` import from lib/utils

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
